search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae early pregnancy bleeding guideline Abu Dhabi
site:dha.gov.ae early pregnancy complications guideline Dubai
site:nice.org.uk NG126 early pregnancy complications recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Treatment and medication recommendations were outside the documentation-mapping scope.
population_applicability: Partial: the source applies to complications through 13 completed weeks, not all uncomplicated early-pregnancy symptoms.
setting_applicability: Partial: the source covers England primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy services; the baseline is setting-agnostic.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; UAE official sources were searched and this current NICE source requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG126 overview and exact recommendation chapters opened on 2026-07-12; last updated and reviewed 2026-06-17.
superseded_check: NG126 is current and explicitly replaces CG154.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports the complication-focused early-pregnancy assessment context, ultrasound and hCG service review, and clinician-arranged escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped pain, bleeding, dizziness, syncope, shoulder-tip pain, pregnancy-test, haemodynamic, and tenderness items.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped ultrasound review and cautions against relying on LMP or a single early scan alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped hCG review, worsening-symptom warnings, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous interpretation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

unresolved_source_gaps: Uncomplicated nausea, vomiting, fatigue, urinary frequency, dehydration, CBC, urine dip, and medication management remain unsupported by the reviewed sections.
Threatened miscarriage and viability-query labels must not be inferred from symptoms.
UAE referral pathways and qualified clinician review remain unresolved.
